title: cerasus hortensis var. serotina
intro: Cerasus hortensis var. serotina, also known as the wild cherry or late-flowering cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, coughs, and digestive issues due to its high content of salicylic acid. The health benefits of Cerasus hortensis var. serotina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support immune system health. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup for medicinal use, and its flowers and leaves are also used in herbal preparations to make jams and preserves.
